student_name: Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari
hub_subtitle: Inglês do dia a dia — apresentar-se, rotina e conversação

lesson.menu_desc: Talking about future plans, goals, and intentions with going to and the present continuous -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 10 -- Plans & Intentions
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari | Lesson 10 -- Plans & Intentions
lesson.grammar_point: future with going to and present continuous

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: something you want to achieve in the future
to plan or mean to do something
to feel excited about a future event
happening soon, in the near future
to plan and organize something in advance
a fixed time to meet someone or do something
to move an event to a later time
the latest time to finish something
to add your name to join a course or event
to find a solution or make a decision
in the future, or coming soon in time

paras: At the start of every year, I like to sit down and think about the months ahead. This year, I have finally made up my mind: I am going to be more organized about my plans. I wrote down three clear goals and I put them where I can see them every day. A goal on paper feels much more real than a goal in your head.

My first plan is about my career. In two weeks, I am signing up for an advanced English course, because I intend to work as a doctor in the United States. The course has a strict deadline for the final exam, so I already arranged a study schedule with a friend. We are meeting online every Tuesday evening. I also have an important appointment with an advisor next month, to figure out the next steps.

Of course, not everything is about work. I am really looking forward to a short trip in the spring, and I am planning to visit my sister in another city. There is one difficult decision ahead: I need to postpone my move to a bigger apartment until I finish the course. It is not an easy choice, but I am sure it is the right one. When I look at the year ahead, I feel nervous and excited at the same time.
reading.source: Alumni by Better -- B1 reading, a learner shares her plans for the year

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: It gives a list of rules that everyone must follow to learn a language.
Ana Paula shares her main plans and goals for the year ahead, for both work and life.
It explains why making plans is a waste of time for busy people.

items: At the start of the year, Ana Paula likes to think about the months ahead.
The text says: 'I like to sit down and think about the months ahead.'
Ana Paula has not decided anything about her career yet.
The text says: 'I have finally made up my mind' and 'I am signing up for an advanced English course.'
Ana Paula and her friend are meeting online every Tuesday evening.
The text says: 'We are meeting online every Tuesday evening.'
Ana Paula is going to move to a bigger apartment before she finishes the course.
The text says: 'I need to postpone my move to a bigger apartment until I finish the course.'

gapfill.text: Two ways to talk about the future. Use 'be going to' for plans and intentions you decided before now (I am going to sign up for a course). Use the present continuous for fixed arrangements that already have a time or place (I am meeting my advisor on Friday). Both talk about the future, but 'going to' is the decision and the present continuous is the appointment in the diary.

bank.label: Useful sentences to talk about your plans
items: This year I am going to be more organized about my plans.
I intend to work as a doctor in the United States.
Next month I am signing up for an advanced English course.
I am meeting my study group every Tuesday evening.
I am really looking forward to my trip in the spring.
I have an important appointment with my advisor next week.
I need to postpone my move until I finish the course.
When I look at the year ahead, I feel excited.

lf.text: Notice the pattern: 'going to' for a plan or intention (I am going to start a new job), and the present continuous for a fixed arrangement with a time (On Friday I am meeting my advisor at three).

items.situation: What is one important goal you have for this year?
tips: This year, my main goal is to ...

items.situation: What are your plans for this weekend? Give a day and a time.
What do you intend to do to improve your English?
Tell me about an upcoming event you are looking forward to.
tips: I am really looking forward to ...

items.situation: Is there a plan you had to postpone? What happened?
tips: I am going to do it when ...

items: Tell me your three main goals for this year. Use 'I am going to' for each one.
Now describe your fixed plans for this week. Use the present continuous with a day and a time, like 'On Monday I am meeting ...'.
Talk about your plans for the whole year with no notes. Mix 'going to' for intentions and the present continuous for arrangements.
A friend invites you out this Saturday, but you already have plans. Politely explain what you are doing and suggest another day.

answerkey.title: Reveal the model answer
list: Model: 'This year I am going to be more organized. My main goal is to pass an advanced English exam, so I am signing up for a course next month. I am meeting my study group every Tuesday, and I have an appointment with an advisor to figure out the next steps. I am really looking forward to a short trip in the spring, but I had to postpone my move to a new apartment until I finish the course.'
Going to for intentions and plans: I am going to be more organized, I am going to sign up.
Present continuous for fixed arrangements: I am meeting my group on Tuesday, I am signing up next month.
Useful future words: goal, deadline, appointment, upcoming, to postpone, to arrange.
answerkey.note: Use 'going to' for decisions and intentions, and the present continuous for arrangements that already have a time or place.

listenings.text: This year I decided to make a real plan for my future. My biggest goal is to change careers, so next month I am going to sign up for a design course. I already arranged everything: I am starting classes in April, and I am meeting my new study group every Wednesday evening. The course has a tight deadline for the final project, so I need to be organized. I have an appointment with my manager next week to talk about my hours, because I intend to study in the mornings. I am also looking forward to a short holiday before it all begins. There is a lot ahead, but for the first time in years, I know exactly where I am going.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sarah! I have some exciting news about my plans. I finally made up my mind: I am moving to Chicago in the summer. I am signing up for a new job there, and I am really looking forward to it. Next week I have an appointment to see an apartment, and I am meeting the landlord on Friday afternoon. I still need to figure out a few things, like the moving date, so I might postpone the trip by a week. Anyway, let us make a plan to have coffee before I go. What are you doing this weekend? Call me back and we can arrange something. Bye!

extra_audio.text: This year I finally made up my mind to plan the future. First, I wrote down my main goal for English. Then I signed up for an advanced course with a strict deadline. I arranged a study schedule and I am meeting my group every Tuesday. Next month I have an important appointment to figure out the next steps.
